System Resource Dashboard
  • app

  • View page source

app package

Submodules

app.models module

System monitoring helpers for CPU, RAM, disk, network, and process control.

app.models.get_cpu_usage(interval=0.5)[source]

Return the current CPU utilization percentage.

Parameters:

interval (float) – Sampling interval in seconds for CPU measurement.

Returns:

CPU usage percentage.

Return type:

float

app.models.get_ram_usage()[source]

Return RAM usage details.

Returns:

RAM usage summary with percent and total memory in gigabytes.

Return type:

dict

app.models.get_boot_time()[source]

Return the system boot time as a datetime object.

Returns:

The time at which the system was last booted.

Return type:

datetime

app.models.get_top_processes(limit=20)[source]

Return the top running processes sorted by memory usage.

Parameters:

limit (int) – Number of processes to return.

Returns:

Process list with pid, name, and memory_percent.

Return type:

pandas.DataFrame

app.models.get_disk_usage(path='/')[source]

Return disk usage metrics for a given filesystem path.

Parameters:

path (str) – Filesystem path to inspect.

Returns:

Disk usage summary with percent used and total size in gigabytes.

Return type:

dict

app.models.get_network_io()[source]

Return network input/output statistics since boot.

Returns:

Network IO summary with bytes sent and received in megabytes.

Return type:

dict

app.models.has_valid_pid(pid)[source]

Validate whether a PID is safe to attempt termination.

Parameters:

pid (int) – Process identifier to validate.

Returns:

True if pid is a valid integer greater than 1.

Return type:

bool

app.models.terminate_pid(pid)[source]

Attempt to terminate a process by PID.

Parameters:

pid (int) – Process identifier to terminate.

Raises:

  • PermissionError – If the PID is protected or access is denied.

  • ValueError – If pid is invalid.

  • FileNotFoundError – If the process does not exist.

Returns:

True if the termination request was issued.

Return type:

bool

app.routes module

Flask route definitions for the system monitor API.

app.routes.kill_process()[source]

Terminate a process by PID.

This endpoint accepts a JSON payload with a single integer property pid. It validates the request body, then forwards the termination request to the service layer in app.models.

— post:

summary: Terminate a process by PID requestBody:

required: true content:

application/json:
schema:

type: object properties:

pid:

type: integer example: 1234

responses:
200:

description: Process terminated successfully content:

application/json:
schema:

type: object properties:

message:

type: string

400:

description: Bad request payload or invalid PID content:

application/json:
schema:

type: object properties:

error:

type: string

403:

description: Permission denied while terminating the process content:

application/json:
schema:

type: object properties:

error:

type: string

404:

description: Process not found content:

application/json:
schema:

type: object properties:

error:

type: string

500:

description: Internal server error content:

application/json:
schema:

type: object properties:

error:

type: string

Module contents

Application package initialization for the Flask backend.
